description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a steep gable roof covered in dark gray shingles and a single chimney on one side. The front facade features symmetrical window placement with four tall, rectangular windows on each floor, accented by white trim and decorative lintels. The main entryway is centered, with a light blue door and a small set of steps leading up to it. The side and rear of the house reveal an attached extension with a flat roof and additional windows, as well as a secondary chimney. The brickwork is consistent throughout, giving the house a classic, sturdy appearance. The overall design suggests a traditional European or early American urban residential style.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical windows, and a central entryway. The house features a rear extension with a flat roof and classic architectural details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade, four windows per floor, central blue door, and single chimney.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the plain brick gable wall, the steep roof, and the attached rear extension with a flat roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the extension with flat roofs, additional windows, and the back door; chimney visible on the main roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the unadorned brick gable wall and the extension's side window.
attribute_entryway_design: Centered single door with steps, flanked by tall windows and decorative lintels, no porch or overhang.
